div.h3: Mechanism for Lock-Free Hash Map Resizing (Shavit & Shalev)
div.p: bit_table.r0_h.class: bucket_0; bit_table.r0_b.class: bucket_0; bit_table.r0_r.class: bucket_0; bit_table.r0_k.class: bucket_0 bit_table.r4_h.class: bucket_0; bit_table.r4_b.class: bucket_0; bit_table.r4_r.class: bucket_0; bit_table.r4_k.class: bucket_0 bit_table.r2_h.class: bucket_2; bit_table.r2_b.class: bucket_2; bit_table.r2_r.class: bucket_2; bit_table.r2_k.class: bucket_2 bit_table.r6_h.class: bucket_2; bit_table.r6_b.class: bucket_2; bit_table.r6_r.class: bucket_2; bit_table.r6_k.class: bucket_2 }

ol.li: Contiguity: All elements belonging to bucket i and its future descendants form a contiguous segment.
Recursive Split: When bucket i splits into i and i+2^k, the new sentinel S_{i+2^k} is inserted via CAS at the boundary.
Sentinels First: By zeroing the LSB of reversed keys, sentinels (bucket heads) always sort before the data nodes they manage.
